retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Care should be taken while polishing. Proper technique should be used to minimize abrasion to the tooth surfaces with the appropriate force, pressure, time, and speed. The least abrasive polishing agent must be used and effective enough to remove the stains. Restorations must be polished with an agent softer than the restoration itself. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Equipment -- Polishing Devices
retrieved_docs.content: Manual: Handheld devices Engine-driven: Require handpieces

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Equipment -- The most frequently utilized is the flour of pumice and calcium carbonate.
retrieved_docs.content: Feldspar: Used on teeth and restorations. Pumice: Used for polishing tooth enamel, gold foil, amalgam, and acrylic resins are siliceous materials. The disadvantage of pumice is its significant abrasive depth and average polishing capacity compared to other polishing agents. Calcium carbonate: Less abrasive than pumice, produces minimum scratches and a highly reflective surface. Perlite: A fluoride-containing abrasive agent. Aluminum silicate is an excellent stain remover with a great taste. It is easy to rinse off, has excellent polishing capacity, and releases fluoride. Xylitol-containing products: Help in saliva production and reduce dry mouth, thus helping to reduce decay, acid, and biofilm production in the mouth. They can be used in kids and are available in various sizes. Novamin containing products: Reduce the sensitivity and help in stain removal. Zirconium silicate: Used on discs and strips and prophylactic polishing pastes. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Polishing pastes contain binders, humectants, flavoring agents, coloring agents, and preservatives to increase patient motivation. They are available in various sizes: coarse, large-sized particles, medium particles, and smooth, small fine particles.

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Extrinsic stains are 1 of the factors that influence tooth color assessment. Performing professional dental prophylaxis before composite or ceramic color selection in anterior teeth restorations could increase treatment predictability. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Technique or Treatment -- Disadvantages
retrieved_docs.content: Air polishers are generally used on supragingival surfaces. Still, glycine powder air polishers are used for removing sub-gingival biofilm, resulting in less erosion of the soft tissues and an 80% reduction in abrasion of the root surface as compared to hand instrumentation or sodium bicarbonate air polishing. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Impression Materials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Zinc oxide eugenol (ZOE) impression material is an irreversible nonelastic material used as a final impression (wash impression) in complete denture fabrication. It records the tissues in the undistorted state. [9] [10] 1. Composition: ZOE impression material is available in 2 collapsible tubes; 2 contain the base and the other the catalyst (see Image . Zinc Oxide Eugenol). The base contains ZnO 87%, vegetable or mineral oils 13%. The catalyst contains eugenol 12-15%, rosin 50%, fillers 20%, resinous balsam 10%, accelerator, and color pigments 5%. *Noneugenol pastes (for patients who are allergic to eugenol) contain carboxylic acids. 2. Instruments and armamentarium: paper pad or glass slab and broad-end spatula. 3. Manipulation: equal lengths are squeezed out from both tubes on a paper pad or a glass slab and mixed with a broad-end spatula until they are mixed to an even color.

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Technique or Treatment -- Air-Powder Polishers
retrieved_docs.content: These are generally used for supragingival plaque removal as they reach inaccessible areas where the rotary devices cannot, such as furcations, flutings, and close root proximities.[16] They use a slurry of water and sodium bicarbonate under air and water pressure, along with certain abrasive agents like aluminum trihydroxide, calcium sodium phosphosilicate, calcium carbonate, and glycine.

retrieved_docs.title: Internal Tooth Whitening -- Technique or Treatment -- Walking Bleach Technique
retrieved_docs.content: A case study describes the application of the walking bleach technique. The tooth was isolated using a rubber dam, and existing composite materials were removed apically around the gingival margin. Sodium hypochlorite was used for disinfection, followed by placing a 2-mm layer of glass ionomer as a protective barrier. Intraorifice barriers are commonly used in the walking bleach technique to ensure proper coronal sealing, which reduces fracture resistance and prevents cervical resorption. [12] [13] [14] Common intraorifice barrier materials include glass ionomer cement, resin composite, and mineral trioxide aggregate (MTA). A damp cotton pellet soaked with 40% hydrogen peroxide was placed intracoronally. IRM was used to temporarily seal the access cavity, after which the rubber dam was removed. The patient was scheduled for follow-up every 2 weeks to assess and repeat the internal bleaching procedure until no further whitening was observed.

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Technique or Treatment -- Polishing Strips
retrieved_docs.content: These are for interproximal regions and line angles of teeth. Since they are very abrasive, the soft tissue in the interproximal areas must be protected. They come in various colors and sizes of abrasive agents.

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Abrasive agents are used for polishing to make teeth lustrous and give smooth and shiny surfaces. They are included in dentifrices and polishing pastes, with the difference that the particles are bigger in the latter.

retrieved_docs.title: Internal Tooth Whitening -- Clinical Significance -- Case Report
retrieved_docs.content: A 28-year-old man with a medical history of controlled hypertension presented with the chief concern, " A previous dentist told me that I need a crown to fix my dark tooth ." The patient underwent endodontic treatment on the upper right central incisor in July 2021 (see Image. Periapical x-ray), with discoloration reported 1 month after root canal therapy. The patient had previously undergone endodontic treatment on the upper right lateral incisor and received a porcelain fused metal (PFM) crown to improve discoloration after root canal therapy. Dissatisfied with the visible discoloration of his central incisor, the patient expressed motivation to pursue a crown, influenced by his positive experience with the treatment of the lateral incisor.

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Click here for a simplified version. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: When polishing pastes with coarse or medium-sized particles are used, they may cause abrasion and damage to the tooth surface by scratching the enamel. A less polished appearance can be seen, increasing bacterial plaque retention. [7] Manual devices are time-consuming, and there is no control over the force applied, and they require more patient compliance. With engine-driven polishers, there is higher aerosol production, heat generation, and damage to the soft tissue if not properly attended.

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: When the nature of dental stains has been identified as exogenous, selective tooth polishing is the initial and most straightforward alternative to remove them after scaling and oral debridement is complete. [7] Dental professionals must know the alternatives when selecting a polishing paste and devices and adjust them to each clinical case.

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Pastes with small particle sizes increase the smoothness and cleanliness of teeth, making them more resistant to plaque accumulation. A polishing agent should be selected so that its hardness is less than the hardness of the surface to be polished. [7]

retrieved_docs.content: ANTIANEMIC PREPARATIONS are classified as vitamin B12 and folic‑acid supplements; they contain both vitamin B12 and folic acid and are used to treat or prevent anemia that results from deficiencies of these essential nutrients.

Recognizing sodium monofluorophosphate as a fluoride source guides oral‑health preventive strategies, while awareness of the SMQ classification aids clinicians in systematic identification and treatment of functional GI disturbances, underscoring the importance of these entities for patient care and product design.

Collectively, these entities illustrate how sodium chloride, sodium sulfate, calcium compounds, a wide range of mineral supplements, bulk compounding chemicals and manganese are organized within pharmaceutical formulations, compounding practices, dietary supplement classifications, replacement therapies and industrial aids, providing a comprehensive framework for patient care, formulation design and safety monitoring.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 2 -- Community 639
retrieved_docs.content: Selenium supplement therapy is a treatment approach that utilizes the substance selenium, with the selenium supplement serving as the active component of this therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is classified as a subcategory within the broader group of Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium, indicating that these health issues are primarily associated with the gestational period and its immediate aftermath.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1783
retrieved_docs.content: Selenium supplement therapy utilizes the substance selenium, referred to as “selen,” as its active component. The Selenium supplement itself is directly linked to selen, indicating that the supplement’s therapeutic effect derives from this element. Selen is classified as a mineral, belonging to the broader category of minerály. Consequently, the Selenium supplement functions as a mineral‑based therapeutic agent, with its efficacy rooted in the use of selenium within the supplement formulation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 1961
retrieved_docs.content: Selenium supplement contains the element selenium and is classified as a form of sodium selenate, indicating that the supplement provides selenium in the chemical form of sodium selenate, which is relevant for its bioavailability and therapeutic use.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1969
retrieved_docs.content: The oral tablet labeled “folic acid 1 MG / vitamin B12 0.5 MG” contains the two active ingredients vitamin B12 and folic acid, providing a combined supplement of these essential nutrients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 2 -- Community 1154
retrieved_docs.content: as active botanical constituents across a wide variety of dosage forms—including sublingual drops, oral liquids, oral pellets, and oral sprays—while ammonium chloride exhibits context‑dependent functionality, acting as an active ingredient in several complex poly‑herbal or homeopathic products and as an inactive carrier in topical shampoo formulations. This underscores the importance of evaluating ingredient roles within the specific formulation to inform safe and effective patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 2568
retrieved_docs.content: Selenium supplement therapy is employed with a therapeutic intent, indicating that its primary purpose is to provide a health‑benefiting effect.
